description_long: This mud patch features a broad, uneven surface with a mix of compacted earth and loose, granular material, likely sand or gravel. The patch is irregularly shaped, with one side bordered by a rough, gravelly edge and the other blending into smoother, lighter mud. Prominently, two long, weathered wooden beams or planks lie partially embedded in the mud, accompanied by scattered smaller debris and stones. The surface texture varies from slightly rippled to coarse, with visible color gradations from pale gray to tan and brown. There are no visible signs of water accumulation, but the area appears dry and firm. The overall scene suggests a neglected or transitional urban space, possibly near a construction site or roadside.
description: A dry, irregular mud patch with gravelly edges, scattered stones, and two long wooden beams lying across its surface.
description_view_0: The mud patch is seen from a low angle, highlighting the two long wooden beams and scattered debris on a mostly flat, pale surface.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the length of the wooden beams and the gravelly texture along one edge, with more visible surface undulations.
description_view_2: The mud patch appears broader and flatter, with the wooden beams running parallel and the gravel edge more pronounced on the left.
description_view_3: Looking from the end, the beams are seen head-on, and the transition from gravel to smoother mud is more apparent.
attribute_location: roadside or construction site in an urban area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with a more defined gravel edge on one side.
attribute_surrounding_debris: wooden beams, small stones, scattered debris
